perturbation.original_text: Vertex shall be responsible for the sourcing/producing of the Product and shall sell to Bunker One, and Bunker One shall purchase, the agreed Output of the Product in accordance to FOB (Marrero, LA, US (or per new/additional agreed Area)) the Incoterms® 2010 rules, at the following scheduled pricing: the arithmetic mean of Platts #2 USGC Pipe and Platt's ULSD USGC Waterborne on agreed pricing days less the agreed upon discount [****].
perturbation.changed_text: Vertex shall endeavor to be responsible for the sourcing/producing of the Product and shall make reasonable efforts to sell to Bunker One, and Bunker One shall attempt to purchase, the agreed Output of the Product in accordance to FOB (Marrero, LA, US (or per new/additional agreed Area)) the Incoterms® 2010 rules, at the following scheduled pricing: the arithmetic mean of Platts #2 USGC Pipe and Platt's ULSD USGC Waterborne on pricing days less a potentially agreed upon discount [****].
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall be' to 'endeavor to be' and 'shall sell/purchase' to 'make reasonable efforts to sell/attempt to purchase' introduces ambiguity, making the obligation non-mandatory. Also, 'agreed pricing days' is replaced with 'pricing days', making the discount potentially not agreed upon.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Uniform Commercial Code - § 2-305. Open Price Term
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text introduces uncertainty about the price term and whether the parties must agree on a discount, conflicting with UCC § 2-305, which requires good faith and commercial reasonableness in determining the price if left open. By making the agreement to the discount 'potential' and softening the responsibility of the parties, the clause fails to provide clear guidance on how the price will be determined, creating a potential breach of contract if the parties cannot come to an agreement on the discount after the contract goes into effect.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-305. Open Price Term. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-305. Open Price Term. (1) The parties if they so intend can conclude a contract for sale even though the price is not settled. In such a case the price is a reasonable price at the time for delivery if (a) nothing is said as to price; or (b) the price is left to be agreed by the parties and they fail to agree; or (c) the price is to be fixed in terms of some agreed market or other standard as set or recorded by a third person or agency and it is not so set or recorded. (2) A price to be fixed by the seller or by the buyer means a price for him to fix in good faith . (3) When a price left to be fixed otherwise than by agreement of the parties fails to be fixed through fault of one party the other may at his option treat the contract as cancelled or himself fix a reasonable price. (4) Where, however, the parties intend not to be bound unless the price be fixed or agreed and it is not fixed or agreed there is no contract . In such a case the buyer must return any goods already received or if unable so to do must pay their reasonable value at the time of delivery and the seller must return any portion of the price paid on account. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modified contract introduces ambiguity regarding the price term and the agreement on a discount, which contradicts UCC § 2-305. The original text clearly states 'agreed Output' and 'agreed pricing days,' establishing a definite obligation. The changed text weakens this by using 'endeavor to be responsible,' 'make reasonable efforts to sell,' 'attempt to purchase,' and 'potentially agreed upon discount.' This introduces uncertainty and makes the obligations non-mandatory. Scraped Law Snippet 1, § 2-305, states that the parties can conclude a contract even if the price is not settled, but subsection (b) notes that the price can be 'left to be agreed by the parties and they fail to agree.' This suggests that if the parties intend to be bound, there should be a good faith effort to agree on a price. The change introduces ambiguity and shifts the obligation to use 'reasonable efforts,' potentially conflicting with the requirement of good faith and a reasonable price at the time of delivery.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party is responsible for payment and reporting of its federal, state, and local income taxes and state franchise, license, and similar taxes required for the maintenance of its business existence.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party should generally consider payment and reporting of its federal, state, and local income taxes and state franchise, license, and similar taxes possibly required for the maintenance of its business existence.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'is responsible' to 'should generally consider' and 'required' to 'possibly required' introduces ambiguity and weakens the obligation to pay taxes.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 26 U.S. Code § 6011 - General requirement of return, statement, or list
perturbation.law_explanation: By changing 'is responsible' to 'should generally consider,' the modified text creates an ambiguity as to whether tax compliance is mandatory. This directly contradicts 26 U.S.C. § 6011, which mandates the filing of tax returns and payment of taxes. The 'possibly required' further weakens the obligation, potentially leading to non-compliance with federal tax law. The text makes taxes an option rather than a legal imperative.

General requirement of return, statement, or list (a) General rule When required by regulations prescribed by the Secretary any person made liable for any tax imposed by this title, or with respect to the
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the obligation to pay taxes by changing 'is responsible' to 'should generally consider' and 'required' to 'possibly required,' introducing ambiguity about the mandatory nature of tax compliance. This contradicts 26 U.S.C. § 6011, which, as indicated in Scraped Law Snippet 1, generally requires the filing of tax returns and payment of taxes by any person liable for any tax imposed by this title. The original text makes tax payment a clear responsibility, while the changed text makes it discretionary. This falls under the Ambiguity Legal Contradiction, where the ambiguous obligation makes it difficult to enforce tax compliance, potentially leading to non-compliance with federal tax law.
